questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A town's population of $8000$ grows by $5\%$ each year. After $2$ years the population is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compound growth multiplies by $(1.05)$ each year.

**Working.** $8000\times(1.05)^2=8000\times1.1025=8820$.

**Answer.** $8820$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The idiom in 'The new exploration plan was a shot in the dark' means:
options: a wild guess with little chance of success
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Idioms carry a figurative meaning distinct from their literal words.

**Working.** 'A shot in the dark' is an attempt made without enough information — essentially a wild guess.

**Answer.** a wild guess with little chance of success.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: For the same $3\times3\times3$ painted cube cut into $27$ small cubes, the number with no painted face is _____ .

figure.caption: A 3 by 3 by 3 cube with the single hidden central cube indicated.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='30' y='50' width='90' height='90' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.06)' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='50' x2='60' y2='140' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='90' y1='50' x2='90' y2='140' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='120' y2='80' stroke='#334155'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='120' y2='110' stroke='#334155'/><rect x='60' y='80' width='30' height='30' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unpainted cubes are the interior ones, forming a $(3-2)^3$ block.

**Working.** $(3-2)^3 = 1^3 = 1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In the right triangle shown, the hypotenuse is $13$ and one leg is $5$. The other leg is _____ .

figure.caption: Right triangle with hypotenuse 13, one leg 5, and the other leg unknown.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='40,140 170,140 40,40' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.10)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='40' y='128' width='12' height='12' fill='none' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1'/><text x='100' y='155' font-size='12' fill='#ef4444'>?</text><text x='20' y='95' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>5</text><text x='112' y='85' font-size='12' fill='#334155'>13</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pythagoras: leg $=\sqrt{c^2-a^2}$.

**Working.** $\sqrt{13^2-5^2}=\sqrt{169-25}=\sqrt{144}=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A company's headcount by department is A $30$, B $50$, C $20$ (see bar chart). Department B's share of the total workforce is _____ % .

figure.caption: Bar chart of department headcounts: A 30, B 50, C 20.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='50' y='100' width='30' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='30' height='100' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='150' y='120' width='30' height='40' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='60' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>A</text><text x='110' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>B</text><text x='160' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>C</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share $=\dfrac{\text{part}}{\text{total}}\times100$.

**Working.** Total $=30+50+20=100$; B's share $=\dfrac{50}{100}\times100 = 50\%$.

**Answer.** $50\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt and appropriate word: 'The new regulation will ____ the older guidelines.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Supersede' (to replace and render obsolete) is the correct word and spelling.

**Working.** 'Supercede' is a misspelling; 'surpass' means to exceed, and 'suppress' means to subdue — neither fits 'replace guidelines'.

**Answer.** supersede.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one interior angle is $70^\circ$ (see figure), its co-interior (same-side) angle is

figure.caption: Two parallel lines crossed by a transversal; one interior angle is marked 70 degrees.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='200' y2='60' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='60' y1='30' x2='160' y2='150' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><text x='95' y='78' font-size='11' fill='#ef4444'>70</text><text x='118' y='112' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>?</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Co-interior (same-side interior) angles between parallel lines are supplementary.

**Working.** $180^\circ-70^\circ=110^\circ$.

**Answer.** $110^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scale the ratio by the same factor.

**Working.** $x=12=3\times 4$, so $y=4\times 4 = 16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: DROUGHT : WATER :: FAMINE : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'crisis : the resource that is scarce'.

**Working.** A drought is a severe shortage of water; a famine is a severe shortage of food.

**Answer.** food.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The capital letter shown, rotated $180^\circ$ in the plane, looks like

figure.caption: The capital letter N.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='30' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>N</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A letter with $180^\circ$ rotational symmetry is unchanged.

**Working.** 'N' has point symmetry, so rotating $180^\circ$ leaves it looking like 'N'.

**Answer.** N (unchanged).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air flows at a mean velocity of $3\ \mathrm{m/s}$ through the airway of cross-sectional area $10\ \mathrm{m^2}$ shown. The air quantity, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#df)'/><defs><marker id='df' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='62' font-size='10' fill='#2563eb'>v = 3 m/s</text><text x='88' y='118' font-size='10'>A = 10 m^2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Airway carrying an air stream

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=A\,v$.

**Working.**
$$Q=10\times3=30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the layout shown, ore is blasted into sublevels and drawn off while the overlying rock caves to fill the void. The method is:

figure.caption: Sublevel caving
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='#475569'><line x1='30' y1='50' x2='170' y2='50'/><line x1='30' y1='90' x2='170' y2='90'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='170' y2='130'/></g><polygon points='90,40 110,40 130,150 70,150' fill='#cbd5e1' opacity='0.6'/><text x='28' y='168' font-size='8'>caved waste flows over drifts</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Caving methods let the hanging wall collapse.

**Reasoning.** Ore broken from sublevel drifts and drawn under caving waste, which fills the void, is characteristic of sublevel caving.

**Answer.** Sublevel caving.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: In a 100 cm core run, the sum of the lengths of intact core pieces each ≥ 10 cm long is 70 cm. The Rock Quality Designation (RQD), in %, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $RQD=\dfrac{\sum(\text{pieces}\ge10\ \text{cm})}{\text{total run}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$RQD=\frac{70}{100}\times100=70\%$$

**Answer.** 70 %.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The trace of $\begin{bmatrix}5 & 2 & 1\\ 0 & 3 & 4\\ 7 & 1 & 2\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trace = sum of the leading-diagonal entries.

**Working.**
$$\operatorname{tr}=5+3+2=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For a circular slip surface, the resisting moment is $5000\,\text{kN}\cdot\text{m}$ and the driving moment is $2500\,\text{kN}\cdot\text{m}$. The factor of safety is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{M_{resist}}{M_{drive}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{5000}{2500}=2.$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A winding rope has a breaking load of 800 kN and carries a working load of 160 kN. Its factor of safety is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $FS=\dfrac{\text{breaking load}}{\text{working load}}$.

**Working.**
$$FS=\frac{800}{160}=5$$

**Answer.** 5.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Progressive heat-stress disorders in hot mines, in increasing order of severity, are broadly
options: heat stroke, then heat exhaustion, then heat cramps
heat cramps, then heat exhaustion, then heat stroke (a medical emergency)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Heat illness escalates with thermal load.

**Reasoning.** Salt/water loss first causes heat cramps, then heat exhaustion (faintness, collapse), and finally heat stroke where thermoregulation fails — a life-threatening emergency. Control is by cooling, ventilation, hydration and work-rest regimes.

**Answer.** Cramps → exhaustion → heat stroke.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The instrument shown has a water-soaked wick over its bulb (figure). The temperature it reads is:
options: the wet-bulb temperature, which with the dry-bulb reflects the cooling power of humid air
the dry-bulb temperature, unaffected by humidity

figure.caption: Wet-bulb thermometer
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='60' y='20' width='14' height='90' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='67' cy='118' r='14' fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='62' y='95' width='10' height='20' fill='#dc2626'/><path d='M67 104 q14 0 14 14' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb'/><text x='14' y='145' font-size='8'>thermometer with wetted wick</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Psychrometry in hot mines.

**Reasoning.** Evaporation from the wetted wick cools the bulb to the wet-bulb temperature; combined with the dry-bulb reading it gives humidity and the cooling power of the air.

**Answer.** The wet-bulb temperature, which with the dry-bulb reflects the cooling power of humid air.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Underground mine water is generally handled by
options: letting it accumulate at the face
collecting it in sumps/lodgements and pumping it to surface, often in stages
blasting it out with the ore
absorbing it into the backfill only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Water gravitates to low points and is lifted out.

**Reasoning.** Drainage channels lead water to sumps (lodgements) at shaft/winze bottoms, from where pumps lift it to surface — directly or in stages for deep mines — keeping workings dry.

**Answer.** Sump collection and staged pumping to surface.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A haul road rises $30\ \text{m}$ over a horizontal distance of $300\ \text{m}$ (figure). Compute the road gradient (%).

figure.caption: Haul road gradient
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='30,100 200,100 200,40' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='200' y1='100' x2='200' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' marker-end='url(#g)' marker-start='url(#g)'/><line x1='30' y1='112' x2='200' y2='112' stroke='#2563eb'/><defs><marker id='g' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='3' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='205' y='75' font-size='8'>30</text><text x='90' y='125' font-size='8'>run 300</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gradient $=\dfrac{\text{rise}}{\text{run}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{30}{300}\times100=10\%$$

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: From the cross-section shown, with overburden cross-sectional area 400 m² and coal cross-sectional area 80 m² per metre of strike, compute the stripping ratio.

figure.caption: Pit cross-section
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='180' height='70' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='120' y='70' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>overburden 400 m^2</text><rect x='30' y='100' width='180' height='30' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='120' y='120' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle' fill='#fff'>coal 80 m^2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Stripping ratio is overburden volume per unit of coal; per metre strike this is the area ratio.

**Working.**
$$SR = \frac{400}{80} = 5$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{5}$ (5:1).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The integrating factor of the linear ODE $y'+2y=e^{x}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $y'+P(x)y=Q(x)$, $\text{IF}=e^{\int P\,dx}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{IF}=e^{\int 2\,dx}=e^{2x}$$

**Answer.** $e^{2x}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Square pillars 15 m wide are formed with 5 m galleries (20 m centres). The development extraction ratio is ____ (four decimals).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans coupled in parallel against a low-resistance circuit (ideal) deliver
options: double the quantity at the same pressure
double the pressure at the same quantity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel fans add quantities.

**Working.** Each develops the same pressure; quantities sum.

**Answer.** double the quantity at the same pressure.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A hoisting skip lifts $10\ \text{t}$ per trip and completes $30\ \text{trips per hour}$ (figure). Compute the hoisting rate (t/hr).

figure.caption: Headframe and skip
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='60,30 120,30 100,160 80,160' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='12' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='80' y='90' width='20' height='25' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='22' y='175' font-size='8'>skip 10 t, 30 trips/hr</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rate $=$ payload $\times$ trips per hour.

**Working.**
$$Q=10\times30=300\ \text{t/hr}$$

**Answer.** $300$ t/hr.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: With only 4 trucks (loading time 2 min, cycle 12 min), the match factor is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $MF=\dfrac{N\times t_{load}}{t_{cycle}}$.

**Working.**
$$MF=\frac{4\times2}{12}=\frac{8}{12}=0.67$$

**Answer.** 0.67 (shovel under-utilised).

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A 'mineral' in geology is best defined as a
options: naturally occurring, inorganic solid with a definite chemical composition and an ordered crystalline structure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Minerals are the building blocks of rocks.

**Reasoning.** A mineral is naturally occurring, inorganic, solid, homogeneous, with a definite (or narrowly varying) composition and an ordered internal (crystalline) atomic arrangement.

**Answer.** Naturally occurring inorganic crystalline solid.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: An LHD (load-haul-dump) unit
options: is a fixed conveyor for coal
is a trackless machine that loads broken ore, trams it a short distance and dumps it, widely used in metal mines and declines
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The LHD combines loader and short-haul truck.

**Reasoning.** A low-profile, articulated, rubber-tyred machine scoops muck at the drawpoint/face, trams it to an orepass or truck, and dumps — ideal for trackless underground metal mining and decline access.

**Answer.** Trackless load-haul-dump machine.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A bench mark (BM) in levelling is
options: a temporary point that changes each day
a fixed reference point of known elevation relative to the datum (usually mean sea level)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Bench marks anchor elevations.

**Reasoning.** A BM is a permanent point whose reduced level (RL) above the datum is known; levelling is referred to BMs so that all heights tie to a common datum such as MSL.

**Answer.** Fixed point of known elevation.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: To blend $2\%$ and $1\%$ ores into a $1.4\%$ feed, the fraction of the $2\%$ ore required is _____ percent.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Chromite and nickel-copper sulphide deposits associated with basic/ultrabasic intrusions are examples of
options: magmatic (orthomagmatic) deposits formed by crystallisation and gravity segregation within a magma
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Some ores crystallise directly from magma.

**Reasoning.** Early-formed dense minerals (chromite) or immiscible sulphide liquids (Ni–Cu) settle and concentrate in mafic/ultramafic bodies, giving magmatic ore deposits.

**Answer.** Magmatic deposits.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: The beam stage loader (BSL) on a longwall
options: is the roof support at the tailgate
transfers coal from the AFC to the maingate belt conveyor, often incorporating a crusher
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The BSL links the face conveyor to the gate belt.

**Reasoning.** At the maingate the BSL receives coal from the right-angle AFC discharge, sizes it through a crusher, and loads it onto the gate (trunk) conveyor for transport out.

**Answer.** Transfers/crushes coal from AFC to the maingate belt.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: At a depth of 200 m the average overburden unit weight is 25 kN/m³. The vertical (overburden) stress, in MPa, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma_v=\gamma H$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_v=25\times200=5000\ \text{kPa}=5\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** 5 MPa.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: In traverse computation, the 'latitude' and 'departure' of a line are its projections on the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** They resolve a line into rectangular components.

**Reasoning.** Latitude $=L\cos\theta$ is the N–S projection (N positive) and departure $=L\sin\theta$ is the E–W projection (E positive); summing them gives coordinate differences.

**Answer.** N–S and E–W axes.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Compute the vertical in-situ stress (MPa) at the depth shown (H = 400 m, unit weight 25 kN/m³).

figure.caption: Overburden column
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='50' y='20' width='60' height='130' fill='#d6c0a3' stroke='#92400e'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><text x='28' y='170' font-size='8'>H=400 m, gamma=25 kN/m^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Vertical stress $\sigma_v = \gamma H$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma_v = 25 \times 400 = 10000\ \text{kPa} = 10\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{10}$ MPa.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\mathcal{L}^{-1}\!\left\{\dfrac{2}{s^{2}+4}\right\}=f(t)$, then $f\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{4}\right)=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathcal{L}^{-1}\!\left\{\dfrac{a}{s^{2}+a^{2}}\right\}=\sin at$.

**Working.** $f(t)=\sin 2t$, so $f\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{4}\right)=\sin\tfrac{\pi}{2}=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In retreating longwall mining,
options: the face advances away from the shaft into virgin ground
gate roads are first driven to the panel boundary and the face then retreats toward the shaft
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Retreat mining develops the boundary first.

**Reasoning.** Both gate roads are driven out to the far end of the panel in solid coal; the face is then installed at the boundary and retreats back toward the shaft, leaving caved goaf behind in already-developed ground.

**Answer.** Develop to boundary, then retreat toward the shaft.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A longwall shearer cuts a $150\ \text{m}$ face with a web of $0.6\ \text{m}$ in a $3\ \text{m}$ seam of density $1.5\ \mathrm{t/m^3}$ (figure). Compute the tonnage produced per pass (t).

figure.caption: Longwall face
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='50' width='160' height='20' fill='#1f2937'/><rect x='30' y='70' width='160' height='30' fill='#a16207'/><g fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'><rect x='40' y='40' width='12' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='40' width='12' height='30'/><rect x='140' y='40' width='12' height='30'/></g><text x='28' y='125' font-size='8'>face 150 m, web 0.6, h 3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Tonnage $=$ face $\times$ web $\times$ height $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$W=150\times0.6\times3\times1.5=405\ \text{t}$$

**Answer.** $405$ t.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dragline is scheduled for all 8760 h in a year, with an availability of 0.85 and a utilization of availability of 0.75. Its effective operating hours per year are ____ (one decimal).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Operating hours $=$ scheduled $\times$ availability $\times$ utilization.

**Working.**
$$h=8760\times0.85\times0.75=5584.5\ \text{h}$$

**Answer.** 5584.5 h.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Undercutting in block caving is performed to
options: remove a horizontal slice beneath the block, withdrawing support so that the block begins to cave
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Caving must be initiated by removing the floor support.

**Reasoning.** A continuous undercut is blasted at the base of the block; once support is gone, the overlying ore loses confinement and caves progressively upward under gravity and induced stress.

**Answer.** Remove a basal slice to initiate caving.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Two forces of $10\,\text{N}$ each act at a point with $120^\circ$ between them. The magnitude of the resultant is _____ N.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The determinant of the upper-triangular matrix $A=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 7\\ 0 & 3 & 1\\ 0 & 0 & 5\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a triangular matrix the eigenvalues are the diagonal entries, and the determinant is their product.

**Working.**
$$\det(A)=2\times 3\times 5=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A caving column is 100 m high. If 80% of the column is drawn as ore before dilution becomes excessive, the ore-column height drawn, in m, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Drawn height $=$ draw fraction $\times$ column height.

**Working.**
$$h=0.8\times100=80\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** 80 m.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Roof bolts are set on a 1.5 m × 1.5 m square grid. How many bolts are needed to support a roof area of 90 m² (as shown)?

figure.caption: Bolt coverage
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='120' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='130' cy='50' r='3'/><circle cx='50' cy='90' r='3'/><circle cx='90' cy='90' r='3'/><circle cx='130' cy='90' r='3'/></g><text x='30' y='152' font-size='8'>area 90 m^2, grid 1.5x1.5 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Number of bolts = roof area ÷ area served per bolt (spacing²).

**Working.**
$$N = \frac{90}{1.5 \times 1.5} = \frac{90}{2.25} = 40$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{40}$ bolts.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Ore value is $\textsf{Rs}\,50$/t and ore mining + processing cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,30$/t, while waste removal costs $\textsf{Rs}\,4$/t. Compute the break-even (maximum allowable) stripping ratio (t waste per t ore).

figure.caption: Break-even stripping ratio
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,40 220,40 180,95 60,95' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='80' y='95' width='80' height='25' fill='#92400e' stroke='#475569'/><text x='80' y='30' font-size='8'>value 50, ore cost 30, waste 4/t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** BESR $=\dfrac{\text{ore value}-\text{ore cost}}{\text{waste cost per t}}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{BESR}=\frac{50-30}{4}=5$$

**Answer.** $5:1$.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A dumper holds 18 m³ (loose) and the shovel delivers 4.5 m³ loose per pass. The number of dipper passes to fill the dumper is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Passes $=\dfrac{\text{truck loose capacity}}{\text{loose load per pass}}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{18}{4.5}=4$$

**Answer.** 4 passes.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The cut-off grade of a deposit is
options: the grade that just separates ore from waste (break-even grade)
the highest grade in the deposit
the average grade of the ore
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Below the cut-off, material is uneconomic.

**Working.** It is the break-even grade dividing ore from waste.

**Answer.** the grade that just separates ore from waste.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Expressed per hour, the instantaneous cutting rate of 13.44 t/min is ____ t/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply per-minute rate by 60.

**Working.**
$$\dot Q=13.44\times60=806.4\ \text{t/h}$$

**Answer.** 806.4 t/h.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A regulator (see figure) must drop 50 Pa while passing $Q=5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. The resistance of the regulator, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='120' y='40' width='8' height='40' fill='#b45309'/><text x='96' y='30' font-size='9'>regulator</text><line x1='30' y1='90' x2='200' y2='90' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#rf)'/><defs><marker id='rf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='34' y='112' font-size='9' fill='#2563eb'>Q = 5 m^3/s, drop 50 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Airway with a regulator

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R=\dfrac{H}{Q^2}$.

**Working.**
$$R=\frac{50}{5^2}=\frac{50}{25}=2\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$$

**Answer.** $2\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the stress state $\sigma_1=80\ \text{MPa}$, $\sigma_3=20\ \text{MPa}$, compute the shear stress on the plane whose normal makes $\theta=30^\circ$ with $\sigma_1$ (MPa).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau=\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}\sin2\theta$.

**Working.**
$$\tau=30\sin60^\circ=30(0.8660)=25.98\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $25.98$ MPa.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: 1000 t of ore at 5% metal is diluted by 100 t of barren (0%) waste. The diluted (delivered) grade, in %, is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Diluted grade $=\dfrac{\text{metal mass}}{\text{total mass}}$.

**Working.**
$$g=\frac{1000\times5}{1000+100}=\frac{5000}{1100}=4.55\%$$

**Answer.** 4.55%.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Each BWE bucket discharges 0.5 m³ (loose). With 48 discharges per minute, the theoretical loose output, in m³/min, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Output $=$ discharges/min $\times$ bucket capacity.

**Working.**
$$Q=48\times0.5=24\ \text{m}^3/\text{min}$$

**Answer.** 24 m³/min.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: A project needs $\textsf{Rs}\,100{,}000$ now and returns $\textsf{Rs}\,60{,}000$ at the end of each of the next $2$ years. At a discount rate of $10\%$, compute the net present value ($\textsf{Rs}$).

figure.caption: Project cash-flow diagram
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='220' y2='80' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='80' x2='40' y2='120' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ar)'/><g stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'><line x1='110' y1='80' x2='110' y2='45' marker-end='url(#ar)'/><line x1='180' y1='80' x2='180' y2='45' marker-end='url(#ar)'/></g><defs><marker id='ar' markerWidth='7' markerHeight='7' refX='4' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L5 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='20' y='135' font-size='8'>-100k now, +60k, +60k</text></svg>

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Which of the following are caving methods of underground mining?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Caving methods deliberately induce the rock mass to collapse.

**Reasoning.** Block caving, sublevel caving and top slicing all rely on caving of ore and/or overlying waste. Cut-and-fill is a supported (filled) method, not a caving one.

**Answer.** Block caving, sublevel caving and top slicing.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench block is 200 m long, 30 m wide and 12 m high. Its in-situ volume, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Block volume $=L\times W\times H$.

**Working.**
$$V=200\times30\times12=72{,}000\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 72,000 m³.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Which of the following are naturally supported (open) methods that require strong ore and/or wall rock?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Open methods leave the opening self-supporting during mining.

**Reasoning.** Sublevel stoping, shrinkage stoping and room-and-pillar all rely on the inherent strength of the ground (and/or pillars/broken ore). Cut-and-fill is an artificially supported method using backfill.

**Answer.** Sublevel stoping, shrinkage stoping and room-and-pillar.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A pillar is $4\,\text{m}$ wide and $2\,\text{m}$ high. Its width-to-height ratio is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $W_p/H_p$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{4}{2}=2.$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: For the same face, the coal produced per shearer cut, in t, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Per-cut tonnage $=$ face length $\times$ web $\times$ height $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$Q=200\times0.8\times2\times1.4=448\ \text{t/cut}$$

**Answer.** 448 t.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The minimum number of discontinuity planes that must intersect to define a rock wedge for wedge sliding is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A wedge is bounded by two intersecting planes (plus the face).

**Working.** Their intersection forms the sliding line.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: An early indication of incipient spontaneous heating underground is
options: a fall in carbon monoxide to zero
the appearance of carbon monoxide, a rise in CO make, sweating of surfaces and a musty/oily smell
a sudden drop in air temperature
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Oxidation produces CO and moisture before flames appear.

**Reasoning.** Rising CO concentration (and CO make), condensation/sweating on surfaces, a haze and a characteristic musty/oily odour are the recognised early warnings of heating, allowing action before open fire.

**Answer.** CO appearance, sweating and musty smell.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Using the cash-flow timeline shown, compute the present value (₹) of ₹100 received at the end of each of the next two years at a discount rate of 10% per year.

figure.caption: Cash-flow timeline
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='220' y2='60' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='3'/><circle cx='120' cy='60' r='3'/><circle cx='200' cy='60' r='3'/></g><text x='36' y='50' font-size='9'>0</text><text x='116' y='50' font-size='9'>1</text><text x='196' y='50' font-size='9'>2</text><line x1='120' y1='60' x2='120' y2='38' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#na)'/><line x1='200' y1='60' x2='200' y2='38' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#na)'/><defs><marker id='na' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='0' orient='auto'><path d='M0 6 L4 0 L8 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='150' y='95' font-size='9'>100 each year, i=10%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discount each cash flow to year 0 and sum.

**Working.**
$$PV = \frac{100}{1.1} + \frac{100}{1.1^2} = 90.91 + 82.64 = 173.55$$

**Answer.** PV $\approx$ ₹**173.55**.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A perpetuity is
options: a loan repaid in two installments
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Its present worth is $A/i$.

**Working.** It is a uniform series with no end.

**Answer.** an annuity that continues indefinitely.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A pit with an overall stripping ratio of 3 will recover 10 Mt of ore. The waste to be removed, in Mt, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Waste $=SR\times$ ore.

**Working.**
$$W=3\times10=30\ \text{Mt}$$

**Answer.** 30 Mt.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A bench block is $200\ \text{m}$ long, $30\ \text{m}$ wide and $15\ \text{m}$ high (figure). Compute the in-place volume ($\mathrm{m^3}$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $V=L\times W\times H$.

**Working.**
$$V=200\times30\times15=90000\ \mathrm{m^3}$$

**Answer.** $90000\ \mathrm{m^3}$.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Periodic 'weighting' on a longwall face refers to
options: the daily tonnage weighed at the surface
the cyclic increase in support load as the main roof spans, breaks and caves at regular intervals
the weight of the shearer drum
the mass of explosives used per round
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The main roof caves in periodic steps behind the face.

**Reasoning.** As the face advances, the main roof cantilever lengthens until it breaks; just before each break the supports feel a load surge (weighting). The first (main) fall occurs at a larger span; subsequent falls recur at the periodic weighting interval.

**Answer.** Cyclic support-load surges as the main roof breaks and caves.
